name: Project.Process.Token.Processing_Logic
observations: NodeManager.scan_log_files processes log files and matches them to tokens
FBC token IDs are zero-padded to 3 digits when they are numeric
Token matching uses multiple strategies: exact match, substring match, and closest alphanumeric match
New FBC tokens can be created if not found in existing node tokens
scan_log_files method processes FBC files and tries to match them to existing tokens using multiple strategies
Strategy 1: Exact token ID match (case-insensitive)
Strategy 2: Token ID substring match
Strategy 3: Closest alphanumeric token match for same token type
If no matching FBC token is found, a new FBC token is created and added to the node
FBC token IDs are normalized by zero-padding numeric IDs to 3 digits

name: Project.ImplementationPattern.Build.PyInstallerBundledExecutablePath_Resolution
observations: Pattern for resolving paths to bundled executables within a PyInstaller package.
Pattern: Detect PyInstaller frozen environment using `sys.frozen` attribute.
Path Resolution: Use `os.path.dirname(sys.executable)` to get the base path of the bundled application.

Path Propagation: Pass the resolved executable path through the application's UI component hierarchy (main.py -> gui.py -> commander_window.py -> commander_ui_factory.py -> session_view.py -> bstool_tab.py) using constructor parameters.
UI Integration: Pre-fill `bstool_path_edit` in `BsToolTab` with the resolved path.

Integration Point 3: NodeTreePresenter - Add new slot to connect to extended CommandQueue.command_completed signal.
Integration Point 4: NodeTreePresenter slot logic - Identify QTreeWidgetItem using NodeToken and update its background color.
Thread Safety: PyQt signals/slots ensure GUI updates occur on the main thread.

Proposed Solution: Extend CommandQueue.command_completed signal, modify CommandQueue._handle_worker_finished to include file write status, and create a new slot in NodeTreePresenter to handle the updated signal and change QTreeWidgetItem color.

name: Project.DesignPattern.UI.GUINodeColorUpdate_Pattern
observations: Pattern: Dynamically update GUI node color based on the success of associated asynchronous operations (command execution and log writing).
Key components: CommandQueue, LogWriter, NodeTreePresenter, NodeTreeView.
Mechanism: Signals from CommandQueue and LogWriter are connected to NodeTreePresenter, which manages the combined state and triggers UI updates.
Application: Provides immediate visual feedback to the user on the status of node-related operations.

name: Project.DesignPattern.StateManagement.PresenterMediated_StateManagement
observations: Pattern: Presenter acts as a central mediator to manage the combined state of multiple asynchronous operations and orchestrate UI updates.
Key components: NodeTreePresenter (mediator), CommandQueue (source 1), LogWriter (source 2), NodeTreeView (target).
Mechanism: Presenter subscribes to signals from multiple sources, maintains an internal state (e.g., node_status dictionary), and triggers UI changes only when all relevant conditions are met.
Benefits: Decouples UI from business logic, centralizes state management, improves testability and maintainability.

name: Project.Component.DataModel.Node_Component
observations: Manages node configurations and connections
Mentioned as a core component in README.md

The Node class now stores tokens as a dictionary of lists (Dict[str, List[NodeToken]]) to handle multiple tokens with the same ID but different types. This change was implemented to correctly handle cases where FBC and RPC tokens share the same ID, which is common in the system.

The add_token method in the Node class has been updated to store tokens in lists grouped by token ID, check if a token of the same type already exists for a given ID, replace existing tokens of the same type rather than adding duplicates, and allow multiple tokens with the same ID but different types to coexist.

These changes are validated by the test_same_token_id_different_types_command_execution test case in tests/commander/test_command_execution.py which ensures proper differentiation and handling of FBC and RPC tokens with identical IDs.

observations: Handles context menu operations for the Commander window

Provides interface between CommanderWindow (View) and business logic
Delegates context menu display to specialized service
show_context_menu method handles two types of items: section items (FBC/RPC subgroups) and token items (individual token files)
For section items, it gets all tokens of the specified type for the node and creates a 'Print All' action
For token items, it creates individual actions for each token
The issue is that for FBC subgroups, users expect individual token actions, not just a 'Print All' action
